response:
To determine how many bottles Brittany needs, we first calculate the total number of washes in a leap year, which consists of 366 days. Since she washes her hair every other day, she will wash it approximately 183 times. At a rate of 1/4 ounce per wash, she will consume a total of 45.75 ounces of shampoo throughout the year. With each bottle containing 14 ounces, dividing the total usage by the bottle size reveals that she requires roughly 3.27 bottles, meaning she must purchase 4 bottles to ensure she does not run out before the year ends.

After purchasing the necessary [number] of bottles, Brittany should store them in a cool, dry place to maintain the product's quality. She can keep the extra supply in her [location] for easy access during the winter months when her hair might require more frequent conditioning. By planning ahead with this calculation, she avoids the inconvenience of making an emergency trip to the store in the middle of the year.
